title: Asystasiella neesiana
taxonomy: Asystasiella neesiana is a species of plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Asystasiella, which is a group of flowering plants. The classification of Asystasiella neesiana is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the order Lamiales. This classification indicates its relationship to other plants within its family and genus. Asystasiella neesiana's classification helps in understanding its characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Asystasiella neesiana, commonly known as the "Golden Dewdrop" or "Creeping Oxalis", is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Oxalidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Asystasiella neesiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Asystasiella neesiana is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as tonics and salves.
